title: United Nations Development Programme(UNDP) Remote Internship Opportunities 2026: Fully Detailed Guide to 4 Global Internships in Sustainable Finance, Research, Data, and Digital Innovation
summary: United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) has officially opened applications for four exciting remote internship opportunities for students and early-career professionals interested in sustainable development, finance, research, data systems, innovation, and digital transformation. These internships provide participants with the opportunity to contribute to impactful global development initiatives while working within an international and mission-driven environment. Each role focuses on a specialized area ranging from sustainable finance and ESG research to digital automation and AI-enabled operations. One of the biggest advantages of these internships is their remote format, allowing qualified candidates from different parts of the world to apply and collaborate with international teams. This article provides a detailed breakdown of all four UNDP internship opportunities, including responsibilities, skills required, learning opportunities, and application links. About UNDP The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) is one of the leading global organizations working to reduce poverty, promote sustainable development, strengthen governance, and address global challenges including climate change, inequality, and economic resilience. UNDP operates in more than 170 countries and territories and works closely with governments, institutions, researchers, and communities to advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Through these internship opportunities, participants will gain exposure to: Sustainable finance initiatives Data governance systems International development work ESG and sovereign debt research AI and automation projects Strategic innovation and analytics Application Deadline Deadline: 26 May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early because UN internships are highly competitive and often receive applications from around the world. 1. Research Intern: Data Governance Architecture Overview The Research Intern for Data Governance Architecture will support research activities related to data systems, governance structures, architecture frameworks, and sustainable finance ecosystems. This role is ideal for candidates interested in: Data governance Information systems Research and analytics Sustainable finance Policy development Digital infrastructure The internship offers an opportunity to explore how data systems and governance structures influence sustainable economic and financial development globally. Key Areas of Work Interns may contribute to: Research on data governance models Analysis of data architecture systems Sustainable finance research Information management initiatives Policy and governance frameworks Data organization and reporting support Skills and Interests That May Be Valuable Candidates with experience or interest in: Data analytics Public policy Information systems Sustainable development Research methodologies Governance frameworks Data management tools may be particularly suited for this role. Learning Opportunities Interns may gain exposure to: International development research Sustainable finance systems Global governance practices Data infrastructure strategy Institutional research methods Apply Here Click here for more and to apply 2. Research Intern: Credit Risk Sovereign Debt Overview This internship focuses on research related to: ESG integration Sovereign debt Credit risk analysis Sustainable finance systems Interns will contribute to research exploring how environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors influence sovereign financing and economic sustainability. This is an excellent opportunity for individuals interested in global finance, economics, development finance, and policy research. Main Focus Areas Interns may assist with: ESG-related financial research Sovereign debt analysis Credit risk evaluation Financial trend monitoring Research documentation and reporting Sustainable finance analysis Ideal Academic Backgrounds This role may suit candidates studying: Economics Finance International Development Public Policy Statistics Political Economy Sustainable Finance Professional Skills That Could Help Applicants Helpful skills may include: Quantitative analysis Financial modeling Research writing ESG knowledge Data interpretation Analytical thinking Career Benefits Participants may develop: Sustainable finance expertise International policy research experience Knowledge of sovereign financial systems Exposure to ESG frameworks and methodologies Apply Here Click here for more and to apply 3. Sustainable Finance Analytics Strategy Intern Overview The Sustainable Finance Analytics Strategy Internship focuses on market intelligence, SDG-aligned finance strategies, and strategic partner mapping. This internship is designed for candidates interested in how financial systems can support sustainable development goals through innovative investment and partnership strategies. Main Responsibilities Interns may contribute to: Market intelligence research SDG finance strategy support Partner and stakeholder mapping Sustainable investment analysis Strategic reporting and documentation Research on global sustainable finance trends Why This Internship Matters As governments and institutions increasingly prioritize sustainable finance, professionals with expertise in SDG-aligned investment strategies are becoming highly valuable globally. This internship offers practical insight into: Sustainable investment ecosystems Development finance International partnerships SDG financing frameworks Ideal Candidates Strong candidates may have backgrounds in: Finance Economics Sustainability International Relations Development Studies Business Analytics Valuable Skills Helpful skills may include: Research and analytical skills Strategic thinking Stakeholder analysis Financial literacy Communication and reporting Apply Here Click here for more and to apply 4. Digital Transformation Automation Intern Overview The Digital Transformation Automation Internship is designed for candidates interested in: Workflow optimization AI-enabled operations Automation systems Dashboards and reporting tools Digital innovation Interns will support efforts to improve operational efficiency through digital systems and technology-driven solutions. This opportunity is ideal for individuals passionate about technology, innovation, and process improvement within international organizations. Key Responsibilities Interns may assist with: Workflow improvement initiatives Process automation projects Dashboard development AI-enabled operational support Data visualization and reporting Digital transformation initiatives Ideal Backgrounds This role may be suitable for candidates studying: Information Technology Data Science Computer Science Business Analytics Engineering Digital Innovation Management Information Systems Skills That Could Be Helpful Candidates with experience or interest in: Automation tools AI systems Data dashboards Process optimization Microsoft Power BI Excel Data visualization Workflow systems may have an advantage. Professional Development Opportunities Interns can gain practical experience in: Digital operations AI-enabled business processes International organizational systems Innovation strategy Automation implementation Apply Here Click here for more and to apply Why You Should Consider Applying for These UNDP Internships These internships provide a unique opportunity to: Work remotely with a global organization Build international professional experience Contribute to sustainable development initiatives Develop technical and analytical skills Collaborate with international experts Strengthen your resume for future global careers Because the internships span finance, research, technology, and sustainability, applicants from various academic and professional backgrounds may find a role aligned with their interests. Important Tips Before Applying To improve your chances: Tailor your CV to the specific internship Highlight analytical, technical, or research skills Mention sustainability or development-related interests Demonstrate teamwork and communication abilities Include relevant projects, coursework, or certifications UNDP internships are highly competitive, so strong and customized applications are important. title: Christian Dior Couture CSR Internship 2026: A Prestigious Opportunity in Corporate Social Responsibility and Social Impact
summary: If you are passionate about social impact, women’s empowerment, inclusion, and sustainability within the luxury fashion industry, the Christian Dior Couture Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Internship could be the perfect opportunity for you. Christian Dior Couture is inviting motivated and creative individuals to join its Corporate Social Responsibility team for a 6-month internship experience focused on meaningful impact-driven projects. This internship provides aspiring professionals with the opportunity to work inside one of the world’s most prestigious luxury fashion maisons while contributing to initiatives that create positive social change. Interns will gain exposure to CSR strategy, inclusion initiatives, women’s empowerment programs, and socially responsible business practices in a fast-paced global luxury environment. About Christian Dior Couture Founded in 1946, Christian Dior Couture is globally recognized as one of the most influential luxury fashion houses in the world. The Maison is known not only for exceptional craftsmanship and fashion innovation but also for its growing commitment to sustainability, diversity, inclusion, and social responsibility. Through its Corporate Social Responsibility initiatives, Dior continues to strengthen programs that promote: Women’s empowerment Inclusion and diversity Community impact Ethical business practices Social innovation This internship allows participants to become part of these transformative efforts while learning from experienced professionals within a globally respected luxury brand. About the CSR Internship Opportunity The Corporate Social Responsibility Internship is designed for individuals eager to gain practical experience in impact-driven work within the luxury sector. Interns joining the CSR team will contribute to projects focused on: Women’s empowerment initiatives Inclusion and diversity programs Social impact activities Corporate responsibility campaigns Community engagement projects The internship offers a rare combination of luxury industry exposure and meaningful purpose-driven work. Internship Duration Duration: 6 months The program is structured to provide interns with immersive professional experience over a substantial period, allowing them to develop practical skills and contribute meaningfully to ongoing CSR initiatives. What Makes This Internship Unique Unlike traditional internships focused solely on administration or observation, this opportunity places interns directly within impact-focused projects that align with global conversations around sustainability, equality, and social progress. Interns will have the chance to: Work in a globally recognized luxury fashion house Contribute to socially meaningful projects Gain exposure to corporate sustainability practices Learn how CSR strategies operate in the luxury industry Build professional experience in inclusion and social impact Collaborate with experienced professionals in a dynamic environment Ideal Candidate Profile Christian Dior Couture is looking for candidates who are: Curious and eager to learn Driven and proactive Creative thinkers Passionate about impact-driven work Interested in social responsibility and inclusion Comfortable working in dynamic professional environments This opportunity is ideal for individuals who want to combine creativity, purpose, and business impact within an international luxury organization. Potential Areas of Learning and Professional Growth Interns may gain valuable exposure to: Corporate Social Responsibility strategy Inclusion and diversity programming Women’s empowerment initiatives Stakeholder engagement Internal and external communications Project coordination Social impact measurement Brand responsibility initiatives These experiences can significantly strengthen future careers in: CSR Sustainability Nonprofit leadership Diversity and inclusion Communications Luxury brand management International development Social innovation Why CSR Experience Matters Today Corporate Social Responsibility has become one of the most important functions within modern organizations. Companies are increasingly expected to create value beyond profit by contributing positively to society and addressing social and environmental challenges. An internship like this provides early-career professionals with practical experience in: Ethical leadership Sustainable business practices Social equity initiatives Corporate impact strategies For students and graduates interested in combining business with social good, this opportunity offers highly relevant and valuable experience. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Applicants are encouraged to prepare: An updated CV/resume A tailored application highlighting interest in CSR, social impact, inclusion, and women’s empowerment Relevant academic or extracurricular experiences Tips for a Strong Application To stand out during the application process: Demonstrate genuine passion for social impact work Highlight leadership or volunteer experiences Showcase creativity and initiative Mention any experience related to inclusion, sustainability, or women’s empowerment Explain why working in CSR within the luxury industry interests you Employers increasingly value candidates who combine professional ambition with social awareness and collaborative skills. Career Benefits of This Internship A successful internship at Christian Dior Couture can help participants: Build international professional experience Strengthen their resume with a globally recognized brand Expand industry networks Develop CSR and sustainability expertise Gain practical project experience Improve communication and collaboration skills Increase competitiveness for future global opportunities For many aspiring professionals, internships within major luxury brands can become a gateway to long-term international careers. title: EUNIC Netherlands Internship Vacancy 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for Students and Recent Graduates Interested in European Cultural Collaboration
summary: Are you passionate about arts, culture, communications, and international collaboration? The 2026 EUNIC Netherlands Internship is now officially open for applications, offering students and recent graduates a remarkable opportunity to gain hands-on experience working with European cultural institutes and embassy cultural departments across the Netherlands. This internship is ideal for individuals seeking professional exposure in cultural diplomacy, project coordination, communications, marketing, and European cultural policy. Selected interns will contribute directly to high-impact cultural projects while building networks with international organizations and professionals in the cultural sector. The internship combines practical learning, professional development, and cultural collaboration in a dynamic European environment. Interns will work on projects such as European Day of Languages, Zoöp Connections, Fair P(L)AY, and European Literature Night. About EUNIC Netherlands EUNIC Netherlands is a cluster of European national cultural institutes and embassy cultural departments working together to strengthen European cultural relations and collaborations in the Netherlands. The organization promotes intercultural dialogue, artistic exchange, multilingualism, and innovative cultural initiatives through collaborative projects involving artists, institutions, policymakers, and cultural practitioners. Through this internship, participants will gain insight into how European cultural organizations operate and collaborate across borders. Internship Overview Internship Duration Start Date: 1 August 2026 End Date: 31 January 2027 Total Duration: 6 months Application Deadline Deadline: 25 May 2026 Time: 23:59 Applications submitted after the deadline or incomplete applications will not be considered. Internship Type Part-time internship 3 days per week 24 hours weekly Location Randstad area, Netherlands Combination of: Member institute visits Cultural event participation Home/remote work Working Language English Internship Stipend €550 net per month Travel costs excluded Why This Internship Matters The EUNIC Netherlands Internship is not a typical internship placement. It provides exposure to: European cultural diplomacy International cultural cooperation Creative communications Event coordination Social media strategy Cultural policy discussions Cross-border project management Interns will work with multiple organizations, each with unique operational styles and cultural perspectives. This creates a highly enriching learning environment for anyone interested in international cultural careers. Projects You Will Support Interns will contribute to several important cultural initiatives organized by EUNIC Netherlands. 1. European Day of Languages This project promotes multilingualism and language learning across Europe while celebrating linguistic diversity. 2. Zoöp Connections A creative initiative focused on ecological thinking, sustainability, and collaborative cultural practices. 3. Fair P(L)AY A project dedicated to fairness, inclusion, and ethical collaboration within the arts and cultural industries. 4. European Literature Night An international literary event showcasing European authors, storytelling, literature, and cultural exchange. These projects provide interns with practical experience in organizing and promoting international cultural events. Main Responsibilities of the Internship The internship combines communications, project coordination, and administrative support. Project and Communication Responsibilities Interns will: Support the planning and coordination of cultural projects Assist with marketing and communication campaigns Create content for: Website Newsletter Instagram LinkedIn Help implement the 2026 communication strategy Develop promotional and campaign materials Produce short-form video content and reels Encourage partners and members to submit content Respond to project-related communication requests Administrative Responsibilities Interns will also: Prepare agendas for monthly cluster meetings Send invitations and reminders Take meeting minutes Maintain member mailing lists Share updates with EUNIC Global and partner clusters Support reports and funding applications This combination of responsibilities ensures interns develop both creative and organizational skills. Skills and Qualifications EUNIC Is Looking For EUNIC Netherlands is seeking candidates who are: Proactive Organized Curious about European cultural policy Comfortable working in multicultural environments Strong communicators in English Able to work independently and collaboratively Preferred Academic Backgrounds The internship is especially suitable for: European Studies students Cultural Studies students Communications students Marketing students Project/Event Management students Recent graduates in related disciplines Additional Skills That Are Considered an Advantage Candidates with the following skills may stand out: Copywriting Content creation Social media management Canva or graphic design tools WordPress knowledge Google Workspace familiarity Video editing for reels and short-form content However, applicants do not need to possess every listed skill to apply. Remote Work and Flexibility The internship offers hybrid flexibility. While remote work is allowed, interns are expected to attend: Scheduled office visits Cultural events Cluster meetings Project activities This structure gives interns both flexibility and meaningful in-person professional engagement. Recruitment Timeline Here is the official recruitment schedule: Call Opens: 15 May 2026 Application Deadline: 25 May 2026 First-Round Interviews: Week of 1 June 2026 Second-Round Interviews: Week of 8 June 2026 Final Decision: Mid-June 2026 Applicants should prepare early because the timeline is competitive and relatively short. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Incomplete applications will not be considered. Important Eligibility Information for International Applicants Applicants must be able to provide proof of enrollment throughout the internship period. However, applicants from non-EU countries who hold a zoekjaar visa status are also welcome to apply. This makes the opportunity more accessible to international candidates already residing in the Netherlands under eligible visa arrangements. Benefits of the EUNIC Netherlands Internship Successful interns will benefit from: International cultural work experience Professional networking opportunities Exposure to European cultural institutions Practical communications experience Event management training Social media and digital campaign experience Experience working in multicultural environments Enhanced CV and career prospects in the cultural sector For students and graduates interested in international cultural careers, this internship can become a strong stepping stone toward future opportunities in arts management, diplomacy, communications, and international relations. For more global Internship opportunities Click Here The post EUNIC Netherlands Internship Vacancy 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for Students and Recent Graduates Interested in European Cultural Collaboration appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UN Women WPHF Palestine Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunities for Women-Led CSOs in Peacebuilding, Recovery, and Humanitarian Action
summary: The United Nations Women’s Peace and Humanitarian Fund (WPHF) has officially launched a Call for Proposals in Palestine, opening a significant funding window for civil society organizations (CSOs) working at the intersection of peacebuilding, humanitarian response, socio-economic recovery, and women’s empowerment. This initiative represents a strategic investment in local women-led and women’s rights organizations that are actively contributing to the Women, Peace and Security (WPS) Agenda in Palestine. The funding opportunity is designed to strengthen both institutional resilience and programmatic impact, ensuring that grassroots organizations are not only able to sustain their operations but also scale their interventions in contexts affected by displacement, conflict, and socio-political instability. The initiative is part of the broader mandate of the United Nations Women’s Peace and Humanitarian Fund, a global funding mechanism dedicated to accelerating meaningful participation of women in peace and security processes, while also supporting recovery in fragile and crisis-affected environments. Purpose of the Call for Proposals in Palestine This Call for Proposals aims to support local organizations that are directly engaged with women and young women affected by displacement and conflict in Palestine. The funding is specifically structured to promote inclusive recovery efforts, enhance political participation, and strengthen leadership roles for women in peacebuilding processes. Projects under this initiative should demonstrate a clear alignment with the Women, Peace and Security Agenda, with a strong focus on forcibly displaced populations. These include internally displaced persons, refugees, returnees, and asylum seekers. Funding Streams Available The WPHF is offering two distinct funding streams under this Call: 1. Institutional Funding (USD 2,500 – USD 50,000) This funding stream is aimed at strengthening the institutional capacity of women’s rights and women-led organizations. The goal is to ensure organizational sustainability, operational efficiency, and increased impact in advancing the Women, Peace and Security Agenda in Palestine. Organizations may use this funding to improve governance systems, financial management capacity, strategic planning, human resource development, and operational infrastructure. The objective is to empower organizations to remain resilient and effective in complex humanitarian and post-conflict environments. 2. Programmatic Funding (USD 50,000 – USD 200,000) This funding stream supports the implementation of programmatic interventions that address socio-economic recovery and political participation of women and young women in peacebuilding contexts. Projects funded under this category must focus specifically on forcibly displaced women and young women in Palestine, including internally displaced populations, refugees, returnees, and asylum seekers. These projects will be part of the Funding Initiative on Forced Displacement. Eligible interventions may include livelihood support programs, leadership and advocacy training, psychosocial support services, community peacebuilding initiatives, and capacity-building programs that enhance women’s participation in decision-making processes. Important Application Guidelines Organizations are strongly advised to note that they can only apply once per funding stream. This means an organization may submit one application for institutional funding and/or one application for programmatic funding. However, if applying for both streams, two separate application packages must be submitted, each following the specific requirements and templates provided. All applications must be submitted online through the official application platform, including the completed proposal template and organizational registration certificate. Deadline for Submission The deadline for submission of all proposals is 21 June 2026 at 11:59 PM (Geneva CET). Late submissions will not be considered under any circumstances. Information Session An online information session will be held for prospective applicants on Tuesday, 19 May 2026, from 10:00 AM to 11:30 AM (Jerusalem time). Interested applicants are encouraged to participate in this session to gain clarity on eligibility requirements, application procedures, and funding priorities. Questions and Clarifications Applicants may submit questions regarding the Call for Proposals via email to the same contact addresses. All inquiries must be submitted no later than 17 June 2026, and responses will be provided within a minimum of 48 hours. Final Notes This funding opportunity represents a critical investment in strengthening women-led civil society organizations in Palestine. By supporting both institutional sustainability and targeted programmatic interventions, the WPHF continues to advance its mission of ensuring that women are not only participants but leaders in peacebuilding, humanitarian response, and recovery processes. Organizations working in this space are strongly encouraged to apply and contribute to building more inclusive, resilient, and peaceful communities in Palestine. Deadline: 21st June,2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this enquiry, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. The post UN Women WPHF Palestine Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunities for Women-Led CSOs in Peacebuilding, Recovery, and Humanitarian Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Research Fellow (Leverhulme Trust Project) at University of Southampton: Study Youth Identity, UPSC Coaching, and Social Mobility in India
summary: The University of Southampton is offering an exciting opportunity for an early-career scholar to join a Leverhulme Trust–funded research project titled “Transformational Coaching for Employment: Identity Formation Among Indian Youth.” This Research Fellow position is an academically rich and socially impactful role situated within the sociology of education, with a strong focus on how elite coaching systems shape the aspirations, identities, and mobility pathways of young people in India. This project investigates one of the most competitive and influential educational pathways in India: coaching for the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) examinations. These examinations are widely regarded as a gateway into prestigious public sector careers, including administrative and governmental leadership roles. The research explores how non-formal educational environments;particularly private coaching institutes; affect how young people see themselves, construct ambition, and navigate systems of inequality. At the heart of the project is a conceptual framework known as “coached habitus,” which examines how structured coaching environments influence identity formation and social behavior. The study aims to understand not just educational outcomes, but the deeper sociological processes that shape youth transitions from education into employment, particularly in highly stratified societies. Research Approach and Methodology This is a multi-method qualitative research project combining several advanced and complementary research techniques. The Research Fellow will be deeply involved in: Ethnographic fieldwork in Delhi, India Biographical and semi-structured interviews with students, coaches, and stakeholders Visual research methods to capture lived experiences Policy analysis to connect educational practice with governance frameworks The integration of ethnographic immersion with visual and narrative-based methods ensures a holistic understanding of how coaching institutions influence youth identity formation. The findings are expected to contribute to international academic literature while also informing policy debates on education, inequality, and employability. Role of the Research Fellow The Research Fellow will play a central role in the successful delivery of this project. The position involves leading qualitative fieldwork in Delhi, including direct engagement with participants and systematic data collection. The Fellow will contribute across all stages of the research cycle, from study design and data gathering to analysis and dissemination. Working closely with Dr Achala Gupta, the Fellow will also have opportunities to co-author academic publications, present at international conferences, and engage with policy stakeholders. This role is designed to support the development of a strong, independent academic profile within the global research community. The position is expected to begin in October 2026 and offers a unique platform for early-career researchers aiming to establish themselves in the fields of sociology of education, youth studies, and qualitative research. Candidate Profile The ideal candidate is an ambitious early-career researcher with strong qualitative research skills and a deep interest in education, inequality, and youth transitions. Applicants must demonstrate: A PhD (completed or near completion) in Sociology, Education, Social Policy, or a related discipline Strong expertise in qualitative research methods such as interviews and ethnography Experience in independent research and data analysis Excellent written and verbal communication skills A strong academic interest in education systems, youth transitions, or social inequality Desirable attributes include prior experience conducting research in Indian urban contexts such as Delhi, familiarity with coaching systems or shadow education, and experience with visual or innovative qualitative methods. An emerging publication record will be an advantage. Benefits and Opportunities This position offers a highly supportive and intellectually stimulating environment. The Research Fellow will benefit from: A leading role in a prestigious Leverhulme Trust–funded research project Opportunities for high-impact academic publications Participation in international conferences Mentorship and structured academic career development Engagement with global policy and academic networks Integration into a vibrant interdisciplinary research culture The University of Southampton promotes equality, diversity, and inclusion, ensuring a supportive environment for researchers from all backgrounds. The role also includes access to a generous benefits package, flexible working arrangements, pension contributions, and wellbeing facilities. Application Requirements Applicants are required to submit: A one-page cover letter addressing selection criteria A two-page CV including publication list A draft academic writing sample Applications should be submitted via email or through the university’s online application portal. Deadline for submission: 11:59 pm UK time on the closing date (not explicitly specified in the listing). APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here . For more global opportunities, click here. title: Head of Unit – Outreach and Engagement at the European Institute for Gender Equality (EIGE): Senior EU Leadership Opportunity in Strategic Communication and Stakeholder Engagement
summary: The European Institute for Gender Equality (EIGE) is currently inviting applications for the prestigious position of Head of Unit – Outreach and Engagement (Temporary Agent, AD10). This high-level role represents one of the most influential communication and stakeholder engagement leadership positions within the European Union’s institutional ecosystem. Based in Vilnius, Lithuania, this opportunity is designed for senior professionals with extensive experience in strategic communication, public affairs, and leadership within complex international environments. The application window opens on 30 April 2026 and closes on 1 June 2026 at 13:30 (Europe/Vilnius time). Successful candidates will be placed on a reserve list for future recruitment. About the European Institute for Gender Equality (EIGE) The European Institute for Gender Equality is a decentralized agency of the European Union dedicated to strengthening gender equality across Europe. The agency plays a crucial role in producing reliable data, research, and methodological tools that support EU institutions and Member States in integrating gender equality into policymaking. EIGE also promotes knowledge exchange, stakeholder dialogue, and awareness-raising activities to ensure gender equality remains a central policy priority within the European Union. Role Overview: Head of Unit – Outreach and Engagement The Head of Unit – Outreach and Engagement is a senior leadership role reporting directly to the Director of EIGE. The successful candidate will be responsible for leading a multidisciplinary team of approximately 18 professionals covering communications, stakeholder relations, digital engagement, media relations, knowledge management, and capacity-building initiatives. This role is central to positioning EIGE as the leading EU authority on gender equality and ensuring that its research outputs, data, and policy recommendations effectively reach decision-makers, institutions, and the wider public. Key operational areas include: Strategic communication planning and execution EU-wide stakeholder engagement and institutional relations Media relations and reputational management Knowledge dissemination and impact monitoring Coordination of EU Council Presidency-related communication Oversight of the Brussels Liaison Office Management of publications and digital communication channels Implementation of international cooperation programmes such as IPA Key Responsibilities The Head of Unit will be expected to: Develop and implement EIGE’s overarching communication and engagement strategy aligned with EU gender equality priorities Lead and manage a multidisciplinary communication and outreach unit Strengthen institutional relations with EU bodies including the European Commission, European Parliament, and Council Oversee high-level media relations and strategic communication responses Ensure visibility and dissemination of EIGE’s research and policy outputs Manage financial, procurement, and budgetary responsibilities in compliance with EU regulations Coordinate stakeholder engagement across Member States, civil society, and international partners Represent EIGE in high-level meetings, conferences, and strategic events Drive innovation in digital communication and knowledge management systems Support organizational change, leadership development, and performance excellence within the unit Required Qualifications and Experience Applicants must meet strict eligibility and professional requirements, including: A completed university degree of at least 4 years (or equivalent with relevant experience) At least 12 years of professional experience, including 6 years in management roles Proven experience in strategic communication, public affairs, or stakeholder engagement at EU or international level Strong understanding of EU institutions, decision-making processes, and policy frameworks Established media relations experience and professional networks Excellent written and spoken English (working language of the agency) Strong leadership, diplomacy, and interpersonal communication skills Ability to manage large teams, budgets, and complex projects under pressure Desirable Skills and Experience Background in communications, journalism, European studies, or related fields Experience in EU or international advocacy environments Understanding of gender equality and non-discrimination policies Experience working in multicultural and multidisciplinary teams Working Environment and Benefits The position is based in Vilnius, Lithuania, a modern, safe, and highly livable European capital. The role offers: A competitive EU AD10 salary (approx. €11,000+ monthly gross depending on experience) Tax-free EU remuneration structure Flexible working arrangements including telework Access to continuous professional training and development Multicultural working environment Family support allowances and social benefits High-level exposure to EU policymaking and institutional networks Selection Process The recruitment process includes: Eligibility screening Evaluation by selection committee Written tests and competency assessments Structured interviews in English Reference and background checks Successful candidates will be placed on a reserve list valid for 12 months, with potential extension. Important notes: No supporting documents are required at initial application stage Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted False information leads to disqualification Deadline 1 June 2026 at 13:30 (Europe/Vilnius time) APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here. title: Smart Family Fund Angel Philanthropy Grants 2026: Funding Up to $100,000 for Early-Stage Nonprofits Driving Scalable Social Impact
summary: The Smart Family Fund is currently accepting pitches from innovative early-stage nonprofit organizations across the United States through its Angel Philanthropy initiative. Designed specifically for emerging nonprofits with bold and scalable ideas, the fund offers grants ranging from $25,000 to $100,000 to organizations that are working to create measurable positive impact but may still be in the early stages of proving their effectiveness. Unlike many traditional grantmakers that prioritize organizations with years of operational history and extensive impact data, the Smart Family Fund focuses on identifying promising nonprofit ventures early and helping them grow into sustainable, high-impact organizations. Through financial support, mentorship, and strategic guidance, the foundation seeks to bridge the funding gap that many social entrepreneurs face during their early development stages. The Smart Family Fund is a private family foundation led by three entrepreneurial brothers who share a strong passion for philanthropy and social innovation. Their mission is centered on discovering and supporting emerging nonprofit leaders who are addressing pressing social issues through creative and impactful solutions. The foundation acts as an “angel philanthropist,” similar to angel investors in the startup world, by backing nonprofit organizations before they become attractive to larger institutional funders. One of the key challenges faced by social entrepreneurs is the difficulty in securing funding for innovative but untested programs. Many nonprofits with transformative ideas struggle to access resources because they lack extensive performance data, established donor networks, or a proven operational model. The Smart Family Fund recognizes this challenge and intentionally invests in organizations at this critical early stage. According to the foundation, their funding approach is designed to support nonprofits that have the potential for positive, scalable impact but have not yet demonstrated the efficacy needed for growth-stage funding or long-term sustainability. By stepping in early, the Smart Family Fund helps organizations build credibility, strengthen operations, and prepare for future expansion opportunities. Each year, the foundation provides first-time grants ranging between $25,000 and $100,000. These grants are intended to help organizations refine their interventions, test innovative ideas, scale promising programs, and strengthen their organizational capacity. The foundation also works closely with nonprofits that demonstrate significant progress, helping position them for support from larger philanthropic institutions and long-term funders. To qualify for funding, applicants must meet several core eligibility requirements. Organizations must be US-based 501(c)(3) nonprofits in good standing with the IRS. Additionally, applicants must clearly explain how they intend to demonstrate the effectiveness of their intervention, quantify their potential impact, and communicate how their organization differs from others working in the same ecosystem. The Smart Family Fund supports organizations across multiple sectors, including education, youth development, community development, social innovation, and human services. The foundation prioritizes nonprofits that are tackling social challenges with innovative approaches capable of creating long-term and scalable change. Applications are submitted through the foundation’s online pitch portal. The review process operates on a rolling basis from September through August, while grant decisions and funding allocations are generally finalized once annually in November. This gives organizations flexibility in preparing and submitting their applications throughout the year. Applicants are encouraged to provide a compelling and data-driven pitch that highlights: The social problem being addressed The organization’s innovative solution Expected measurable outcomes Long-term scalability Why the organization stands out from similar initiatives The Smart Family Fund emphasizes simplicity in its application process and aims to reduce administrative burden for applicants while still gathering enough information to assess strategic fit and impact potential. This opportunity represents a valuable funding pathway for nonprofit founders and social entrepreneurs seeking catalytic support at a stage when many traditional funding avenues remain inaccessible. For organizations with visionary ideas and the capacity to generate meaningful social impact, the Smart Family Fund Angel Philanthropy Grants could provide the financial backing and mentorship needed to accelerate growth and sustainability. Funding Details: Grant Amount: $25,000 – $100,000 Application Type: Rolling Applications Decision Period: Typically finalized in November Eligible Applicants: US-based nonprofit organizations Deadline: Rolling applications (Reviewed September–August) Funding decisions are generally made in November. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. title: Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026 in the United Kingdom for Conflict-Affected Students from Kenya and Uganda
summary: For many talented students living in conflict-affected communities, access to quality postgraduate education remains one of the biggest barriers to career advancement and community transformation. The Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026 is creating a life-changing opportunity for refugees and conflict-affected individuals residing in Kenya and Uganda to pursue fully funded Master’s degree studies in the United Kingdom. This prestigious scholarship programme, funded primarily through the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission Master’s and Shared Scholarships schemes, is designed to empower future leaders, professionals, educators, researchers, and changemakers who are committed to driving development in their countries and communities. Applications are officially open from 15 May to 12 June 2026 . About the Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme Windle Trust International (WTI) serves as a nominating partner for the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission (CSC). Through this partnership, eligible candidates receive extensive support throughout the scholarship and university application process. Applicants who successfully pass the WTI selection process will: Receive professional guidance on applying to UK universities Obtain support in preparing Commonwealth Scholarship Commission applications Be considered for nomination by Windle Trust International to the CSC Gain access to tailored scholarship application guidance and support materials The programme aims to support individuals whose education and career opportunities have been disrupted by conflict and displacement while helping them gain advanced knowledge that contributes to sustainable development in their communities. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive a fully funded Master’s scholarship package that includes: Full Tuition Fees The scholarship covers the full cost of tuition at participating UK universities. Monthly Maintenance Stipend Selected scholars will receive financial support to cover living expenses while studying in the United Kingdom. Relocation Costs The programme provides relocation assistance to help scholars transition smoothly to the UK for their studies. University Application Support WTI provides guidance and assistance throughout the university application process. Commonwealth Scholarship Commission Application Support Applicants receive tailored support for completing the CSC scholarship application successfully. Access to Exclusive Scholarship Guidance Materials Shortlisted candidates gain access to: Question-by-question guidance Scholarship application examples General scholarship writing tips The complete Windle-CSC Application Guide Eligible Fields of Study One of the strongest features of this scholarship programme is its flexibility. All subject areas are eligible; however, priority is often given to courses that contribute directly to national and community development. Priority fields include: Education Social Work Development Studies Public Health Nutrition Crop Science Animal Science Gender Studies Renewable Energy Climate Change Development-related Law Agroforestry and Food Security English Language Education Computer Science Engineering These areas are particularly encouraged because of their potential impact on economic growth, sustainability, healthcare improvement, food security, technological advancement, and social development. Where Can Scholars Study? Applicants may apply to universities in the United Kingdom that participate in the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission schemes. WTI strongly encourages applicants to target universities that do not charge application fees. While Windle Trust International has negotiated fee waivers with some universities, fee waivers are not available universally. Importantly: WTI does not pay university application fees Applicants should confirm fee waiver availability before applying Enquiries regarding fee waivers can be sent to postgraduate@windle.org.uk Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026, applicants must meet all eligibility criteria without exception. Applicants must: Have refugee status OR reside in specific conflict-affected areas in Kenya or Uganda Demonstrate excellent English language proficiency Hold a minimum academic qualification equivalent to a British Upper Second-Class (2:1) undergraduate degree Possess at least two years of relevant professional or volunteer experience Applicants are advised to read the complete Eligibility Guidance Document carefully before submitting an application because eligibility requirements are strictly enforced. Application Process Interested candidates must submit applications through the Windle Trust International online application portal. Application Timeline Opening Date: 15 May 2026 Deadline: 12 June 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to begin preparing early. Selection Process The selection process involves multiple stages designed to identify highly motivated and qualified candidates. Step 1: Online Application Applicants submit their applications through the WTI online portal. Step 2: Shortlisting Eligible candidates are shortlisted based on academic merit, experience, leadership potential, and alignment with development goals. Step 3: Interview Shortlisted applicants are invited for interviews either: Online, or In their country of residence Step 4: Scholarship and University Application Support Candidates who pass the interview stage receive: Tailored CSC application support UK university application assistance Guidance materials and mentoring Step 5: Final Scholarship Decision Final scholarship awards are determined by the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission. Commitment to Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Windle Trust International promotes equal opportunity and non-discrimination throughout the selection process. The organization strongly encourages applications from: Women Refugees Minority groups Persons living with disabilities Individuals affected by conflict This inclusive approach ensures that deserving candidates from vulnerable and underrepresented backgrounds have access to quality higher education opportunities. Why This Scholarship Matters The Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme is more than a scholarship. It is an investment in future leaders who can contribute meaningfully to rebuilding communities affected by conflict and displacement. By supporting postgraduate education in critical development sectors, the programme empowers scholars to: Improve healthcare systems Strengthen educational institutions Advance climate resilience Promote sustainable agriculture Enhance technological innovation Advocate for social justice and inclusion Graduates are expected to apply their knowledge and skills toward solving pressing challenges in their communities and countries. Important Notes for Applicants Before applying, candidates should keep the following in mind: WTI never charges fees for scholarship applications Eligibility requirements are applied strictly University application fees are not covered by WTI Applicants should prepare all documents early Strong academic and professional records improve competitiveness For application guidance and enquiries, applicants may contact: Email: postgraduate@windle.org.uk APPLY NOW KENYA APPLY NOW UGANDA Final Thoughts The Windle Trust International Postgraduate Scholarship Programme 2026 represents a remarkable opportunity for conflict-affected individuals in Kenya and Uganda to pursue fully funded Master’s studies in the United Kingdom. Beyond financial support, the programme provides mentorship, application guidance, and academic opportunities that can transform careers and communities. For refugees and conflict-affected individuals seeking to become leaders in development, education, healthcare, technology, agriculture, and sustainability, this scholarship could be the gateway to a brighter future. Applicants are encouraged to prepare thoroughly and submit their applications before the 12 June 2026 deadline . For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . title: AmaliTech Rwanda Paid Apprenticeship Programmes 2026 for Software Development and Data Engineering Professionals
summary: The AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programmes 2026 in Kigali, Rwanda, present an exceptional opportunity for aspiring and experienced technology professionals seeking to build globally competitive careers in software development, data engineering, quality assurance, and DevOps. Through its structured apprenticeship tracks, AmaliTech is equipping African tech talent with practical experience, international-standard training, and direct exposure to real-world client projects. The programmes are designed for two categories of professionals: Early-career talents and recent graduates seeking intensive mentorship and career acceleration. Mid-level professionals with prior industry experience looking to deepen their expertise and transition into globally competitive engineering roles. Both apprenticeship tracks are fully paid and based on-site in Kigali, Rwanda, providing participants with immersive learning experiences under the guidance of senior engineers. About the AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programmes AmaliTech is known for building high-quality digital and technology talent across Africa while connecting professionals to global opportunities. Its apprenticeship programmes combine technical training, project-based learning, mentorship, and exposure to international delivery standards. Participants in the programme work on real-world projects and gain practical skills aligned with global industry expectations. Apprentices are trained in collaborative software engineering environments while sharpening both technical and professional competencies. The apprenticeships are conducted full-time on-site from Monday to Friday in Kigali, Rwanda. Available Apprenticeship Tracks Participants can specialize in several high-demand technology areas, including: Java Backend Development Python Backend and App Development JavaScript Full Stack Development Data Engineering Quality Assurance (QA) DevOps Engineering These specializations are designed to prepare participants for modern technology careers and equip them with industry-relevant expertise. Early-Career Apprenticeship Programme The first apprenticeship programme is a six-month full-time paid opportunity specifically tailored for: Final-year university students approaching graduation Recent graduates Early-career technology professionals The programme focuses on helping emerging tech talent transition into professional engineering careers through hands-on experience and mentorship. What Participants Will Gain Participants in the early-career programme will benefit from: Intensive mentorship from senior engineers Practical project-based learning Exposure to international client projects Industry-standard engineering practices Collaborative team environments Career acceleration opportunities Technical and professional skill development Real-world software engineering experience Eligibility Requirements for Early-Career Applicants To qualify for the programme, applicants must meet the following requirements: Educational Qualifications Applicants should be: Final-year university students approaching graduation, or Recent graduates in: Computer Science Information Technology Engineering Data Science Related fields Technical Experience Applicants must possess at least six months of practical programming experience obtained through: Internship programmes Training programmes School projects Personal projects Hackathons Online courses Freelance or self-learning experiences Additional Requirements Candidates must also demonstrate: Passion for software development Strong interest in long-term technology careers Ability to work independently Initiative and accountability Growth-oriented mindset Adaptability and willingness to learn Strong teamwork skills Excellent problem-solving abilities Effective communication skills Fluency in spoken and written English Applicants must also be: Currently based in Rwanda, or Willing to relocate to Kigali, Rwanda Mid-Level Professional Apprenticeship Programme AmaliTech also offers a second apprenticeship track designed specifically for mid-level professionals. This four-month full-time paid apprenticeship is intended for professionals who already possess industry experience and want to advance into more specialized technical roles. The programme focuses on advanced upskilling and practical exposure to industry-driven projects. Focus Areas for Mid-Level Professionals Participants in this programme will deepen their expertise in: Java Backend Development Python Backend and App Development JavaScript Full Stack Development Data Engineering Quality Assurance (QA) DevOps Engineering The apprenticeship emphasizes advanced technical growth and direct project delivery experience within professional engineering environments. Benefits of the AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programmes Participants selected for either programme will enjoy numerous professional and career benefits, including: 1. Fully Paid Apprenticeship Both programmes are paid opportunities, allowing participants to learn and grow professionally while receiving financial support. 2. Real-World Industry Experience Apprentices gain direct experience working on live projects that support international clients. 3. Mentorship from Senior Engineers Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals who help shape their technical and professional development. 4. Career Advancement Opportunities The programmes are designed to prepare apprentices for long-term careers in global technology industries. 5. International Engineering Standards Participants are trained according to internationally recognized delivery and engineering standards. 6. Opportunity for Full-Time Employment One of the most attractive features of the programme is the possibility of securing a full-time role at AmaliTech after successful completion. What Happens After the Apprenticeship? At the end of the apprenticeship period, participants undergo a structured assessment process. The evaluation is based on several factors, including: Technical competence Professionalism Delivery reliability Collaboration skills Readiness for independent project execution Alignment with international engineering standards Participants who successfully meet the required performance expectations and demonstrate strong professional readiness may receive full-time job offers within AmaliTech. This provides a direct pathway into long-term employment and career growth within an internationally connected technology company. Why the AmaliTech Apprenticeship Stands Out The AmaliTech Apprenticeship Programme is not just another training initiative. It serves as a launchpad for emerging and growing technology professionals across Africa. What makes the programme unique includes: Strong focus on practical learning Real client project exposure Career-focused mentorship Access to international engineering practices Direct pathway to employment Highly in-demand technical specializations Structured professional development environment For aspiring software engineers, backend developers, DevOps professionals, QA engineers, and data engineers, this programme offers a rare opportunity to gain globally relevant experience while building a sustainable career in technology. Application Status Applications are currently open for both apprenticeship programmes. Deadline No application deadline has been specified yet. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible before slots are filled. APPLY HERE Applicants whose preferred specialization may not currently be available are still encouraged to submit their CVs for consideration in future cohorts. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here . title: Fully Funded UK PhD 2026 at Lancaster University on Healthcare Carbon Emissions, Virtual Wards and Patient Care Decisions
summary: Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD opportunity at Lancaster University focused on healthcare sustainability, carbon emissions, patient care decision-making, and virtual ward systems within acute healthcare settings in England. This exciting interdisciplinary doctoral research project is designed for students passionate about health inequalities, public health, environmental sustainability, healthcare systems, sociology, psychology, nursing, and implementation science. The PhD project, titled “Exploring Patient and Carer Understandings of and Perspectives on Carbon Emissions When Making Virtual Ward Care Decisions Within Acute Care Settings,” offers successful applicants a fully funded studentship including an annual stipend of £21,805 per year alongside tuition fee coverage for eligible home students. The research is highly relevant to the future of healthcare systems globally, especially as healthcare institutions increasingly focus on sustainability and reducing carbon emissions while maintaining quality patient care. About the Research Project The National Health Service (NHS) in England became the first healthcare system globally to commit to ambitious net-zero carbon emissions targets: Net zero for direct emissions by 2040 Net zero for broader emissions by 2045 Healthcare delivery contributes significantly to carbon emissions within the NHS. According to the project information: Care provision accounts for approximately 24% of NHS carbon emissions. Acute hospital care contributes around 56% of emissions related to care delivery. Acute care represents approximately 13% of all NHS England emissions. This makes acute healthcare settings one of the largest contributors to healthcare-related environmental impact in England. The PhD project seeks to understand how patients and carers perceive the environmental impact of healthcare and how carbon emission information may influence healthcare decisions, especially regarding virtual ward care systems. Focus Areas of the PhD Research The successful PhD candidate will investigate patient and carer perspectives surrounding environmentally sustainable healthcare and virtual care delivery models. Research Areas Include: Understanding patient and carer awareness of healthcare-related carbon emissions Exploring how environmental information may influence healthcare choices Investigating virtual ward systems in acute care settings Studying experiences related to: Heart failure Respiratory illnesses Frailty-related conditions Co-designing patient guidance on environmentally sustainable healthcare Evaluating implementation strategies and impact What the Successful Candidate Will Do The selected doctoral researcher will undertake several important research and engagement activities throughout the PhD programme. Key Responsibilities Include: 1. Conduct a Systematic Review The student will carry out a comprehensive literature review focused on: Patient and carer understanding of healthcare environmental impacts Carbon emissions associated with healthcare delivery Sustainable healthcare communication 2. Deliver Interviews and Workshops The project involves: Conducting patient and carer interviews Organizing deliberative workshops Analysing qualitative data Gathering experiences from individuals affected by heart failure, respiratory illness, and frailty-related conditions 3. Facilitate Co-Design Activities The candidate will work collaboratively with patients and carers to develop guidance materials related to: Environmental impacts of acute healthcare Sustainable healthcare decision-making Patient-centered communication tools The project will also use theoretical and conceptual models linked to co-design methodologies. 4. Evaluate Implementation and Impact The student will: Design and deliver implementation evaluations Explore real-world application of the developed toolkit Use implementation science approaches to assess outcomes and impact 5. Collaborate with Research Teams and PPIE Groups There will also be opportunities to: Work alongside interdisciplinary research teams Collaborate with Patient Public Involvement and Engagement (PPIE) groups Support public engagement strategies Contribute to evaluation activities Funding and Financial Benefits This PhD studentship offers substantial financial support for eligible candidates. Funding Includes: Fully funded PhD studentship Annual stipend of £21,805 Tuition fee coverage for home students Opportunity to engage in impactful healthcare research Access to interdisciplinary academic supervision and networks The funding duration aligns with standard UK doctoral programme structures. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the academic qualifications required for admission. Applicants Should Have: Undergraduate Degree A strong first degree (minimum 2:1 classification or equivalent) in a relevant discipline such as: Nursing Psychology Sociology Public health Medical humanities Social sciences Health sciences Master’s Degree A Master’s degree with at least Merit level (or equivalent) in a relevant social science or healthcare-related discipline is preferred. Important Dates Application Deadline 15 June 2026 Interview Period Week commencing 29 June 2026 Programme Start Date 28 September 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early to avoid missing the deadline. Apply Here Supervisors and Contact Information Prospective applicants may contact the project supervisors for further discussion or clarification regarding the research proposal and application process. Supervisors Professor Heather Brown Professor Amanda Taylor-Beswick Why This PhD Opportunity Stands Out This PhD offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of: Healthcare sustainability Environmental policy Patient-centred care Public health Health inequalities Implementation science Virtual healthcare systems The project is especially relevant in today’s global healthcare environment where sustainability and carbon reduction strategies are becoming increasingly important. Students interested in climate-conscious healthcare systems and socially impactful research will find this doctoral opportunity highly valuable. Career Opportunities After the PhD Graduates from this programme may pursue careers in: Health policy research Public health institutions NHS research and innovation Academia and teaching Healthcare sustainability consulting Health economics Implementation science Environmental health policy Patient engagement and healthcare innovation Final Thoughts As healthcare systems worldwide seek sustainable and environmentally responsible models of care delivery, research opportunities like this fully funded Lancaster University PhD are becoming increasingly significant. This programme not only provides strong financial support but also allows students to contribute to globally relevant healthcare transformation and sustainability goals.
